events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the required JSON directly. This is a compliance determination, so I’m treating thick backfill lifts as a failed condition because the specified compaction method cannot reliably achieve required density.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.9,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Backfill lifts are too thick for the compactor being used.\",
    \"Required bedding and backfill compaction cannot be verified or reliably achieved in the observed lift thickness.\",
    \"Condition does not comply with project specifications for controlled backfill placement and compaction.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop covering the affected pipe run until the backfill condition is corrected and inspected.\",
    \"Remove or rework over-thick lifts to the specified loose lift thickness suitable for the compaction equipment.\",
    \"Recompact bedding and backfill in compliant lifts and obtain required field density testing or inspection acceptance before proceeding.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Backfill placed in lifts thicker than the compactor can properly compact is non-compliant because it can leave voids, settlement risk, poor pipe support, and inadequate protection of the pipe. IPC 306 requires underground building drain and sewer piping to be installed with proper bedding, support, and backfill, and the project specifications govern lift thickness and compaction requirements.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"Project specifications: pipe bedding, backfill lift thickness, and compaction requirements\",
    \"IPC 306: trenching, bedding, pipe support, and backfill for underground drainage piping\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json && cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
